messages.content:
You are an expert at classifying job postings for Remote EU eligibility. A Remote EU position must be FULLY REMOTE and allow work from EU member countries. Return structured JSON output with clear reasoning.

CLASSIFICATION RULES (apply in order):

0. NEGATIVE SIGNALS (highest priority -- override all other rules):
   - "US only", "must be based in US", "US work authorization required" -> isRemoteEU: false (high confidence)
   - "No EU applicants", "cannot accept from EU" -> isRemoteEU: false (high confidence)
   - Swiss-only work permit in DACH context -> isRemoteEU: false (high confidence)

1. FULLY REMOTE REQUIREMENT: Must explicitly state "remote", "fully remote", or similar.
   - Hybrid, office-based, or on-site positions -> isRemoteEU: false
   - "Remote support" (providing remote support from an office) is NOT remote work

2. ATS METADATA SHORTCUTS (when available):
   - EU country code + ATS remote flag -> isRemoteEU: true (high confidence)
   - ATS workplace_type = "not remote" -> isRemoteEU: false (high confidence)

3. EXPLICIT EU MENTIONS: "Remote - EU", "EU only", "EU members only" -> isRemoteEU: true (high confidence)

4. WORK AUTHORIZATION: "EU work authorization", "EU passport", "EU residency" -> isRemoteEU: true

5. REGIONAL SHORTHANDS:
   - "DACH" -> isRemoteEU: true (medium -- 2 of 3 are EU)
   - "Nordics" -> isRemoteEU: true (medium -- 3 of 5 are EU)
   - "Benelux" -> isRemoteEU: true (high -- all EU)

6. BROADER REGIONS:
   - "EMEA" + EU work auth -> isRemoteEU: true (high)
   - "EMEA" alone -> isRemoteEU: true (medium)
   - "Europe" without EU specification -> isRemoteEU: true (medium)

7. TIMEZONE SIGNALS:
   - "EU Timezone" -> isRemoteEU: true (medium)
   - "CET +/- N hours" -> isRemoteEU: true (medium)
   - "CET timezone" alone (no EU context) -> isRemoteEU: false (medium)

8. WORLDWIDE / GLOBAL:
   - Worldwide + negative signals -> isRemoteEU: false (high)
   - Worldwide + EU country/office/timezone -> isRemoteEU: true (medium)
   - Worldwide + no EU specifics -> isRemoteEU: false (medium)

9. UK only (post-Brexit) -> isRemoteEU: false. Switzerland only -> isRemoteEU: false.

10. CONFIDENCE: HIGH = explicit EU mention, clear remote, work auth, all-EU region, negative signals. MEDIUM = mixed regions, EEA, Europe, EMEA, DACH, Nordics, EU timezone. LOW = vague, worldwide with no EU specifics.

Classify this job posting as Remote EU or not.

JOB DETAILS:
- Title: Senior Backend Engineer
- Location: Remote - EU
- Description: We are hiring across all EU countries. Must have EU work authorization. Work from anywhere in the European Union. Competitive salary, 30 days PTO, home office budget.

STRUCTURED SIGNALS:
- ATS remote flag: YES
- No structured ATS signals available

{"isRemoteEU": true, "confidence": "high", "reason": "Explicitly states 'Remote - EU' in location, requires EU work authorization, and describes work from anywhere in the EU. Rule 3 (explicit EU mention) + Rule 4 (EU work auth) apply."}

Classify this job posting as Remote EU or not.

JOB DETAILS:
- Title: Product Designer
- Location: Remote
- Description: Fully remote position. Must be based in the United States. US citizens and permanent residents only. We do not hire outside the United States. Competitive salary, 401(k), medical, dental, and vision insurance.

STRUCTURED SIGNALS:
- ATS remote flag: YES
- Negative signals: must be based in the united states, us citizens and permanent residents only

{"isRemoteEU": false, "confidence": "high", "reason": "Rule 0 (negative signals) applies: explicit US-only requirement ('must be based in the United States', 'US citizens and permanent residents only') overrides all other signals. US benefits (401k, medical/dental/vision) further confirm US-only position."}

Classify this job posting as Remote EU or not.

JOB DETAILS:
- Title: Senior Design Engineer (Remote) @ Peec AI
- Location: Worldwide (+-3 hours CET)
- Description: Take ownership of complex frontend features end-to-end. One of Europe's fastest-growing Series A startups. Remote working (applicants must be located within +-3 hours of the Berlin (CET) time).

STRUCTURED SIGNALS:
- ATS remote flag: YES
- EU timezone/business hours signal detected

{"isRemoteEU": true, "confidence": "medium", "reason": "Rule 7 (timezone signals) applies: CET +/- 3 hours requirement in a worldwide role targets EU-compatible workers. Rule 8 applies: worldwide + EU timezone signal = true (medium). European Series A startup based in Berlin further supports EU eligibility."}

Classify this job posting as Remote EU or not.

JOB DETAILS:
- Title: Software Engineer
- Location: Berlin, Germany (Hybrid)
- Description: 2-3 days per week in office in Berlin, Germany. EU candidates preferred. Must be willing to come to the office regularly. Great team culture and on-site perks.

STRUCTURED SIGNALS:
- Country code: DE (EU member)
- EU countries in location: germany

{"isRemoteEU": false, "confidence": "high", "reason": "Rule 1 (fully remote requirement) fails: position is hybrid (2-3 days in office per week). Despite EU country code (DE) and EU candidate preference, the role is not fully remote. Hybrid and office-based positions are classified as not Remote EU."}

Classify this job posting as Remote EU or not.

JOB DETAILS:
- Title: Frontend Engineer
- Location: Remote - Netherlands
- Description: Fully remote position based in the Netherlands. We are a Dutch company offering competitive local benefits. EU work permit required. 25 days holiday, pension contribution, home office stipend. Salary: 70,000-90,000 EUR/year.

STRUCTURED SIGNALS:
- ATS remote flag: YES
- Country code: NL (EU member)
- EU countries in location: netherlands

{"isRemoteEU": true, "confidence": "high", "reason": "Rule 2 (ATS metadata shortcut) applies: EU country code NL + ATS remote flag = high confidence. Additionally, Rule 3 (explicit EU mention via Netherlands) and Rule 4 (EU work permit required) reinforce classification. EUR salary and Dutch benefits confirm EU-based position."}
